prompt: A nostalgic cinematic photograph of a small old-fashioned Chinese general store interior, shot from customer eye level with a slightly wide framing. Warm sepia and amber tones, soft window light from the left, subtle film grain, faint vignette, and a slightly hazy vintage look. Behind a long dark wooden-and-glass display counter stands 1 shopkeeper, a woman with a short black bob haircut wearing a plain white short-sleeve blouse, shown in side profile while working with her hands at the counter. The store feels authentic, modest, and lived-in, like the late 1970s to 1990s. On the right side, a black vintage sewing machine sits on an upper shelf with a handwritten price sign in Chinese beside it reading “768元”. Behind it are shelves packed with small colorful product boxes and tins. On the wall hang 1 large vertical handwritten price list in Chinese calligraphy and 2 old paper calendars or posters. The foreground glass cases display many old packaged goods: stacks of colorful tins and jars, several rectangular illustrated tins, 2 enamel wash basins with floral patterns, and multiple pairs of plain white canvas shoes arranged side by side on the bottom shelf. The left side of the frame shows bright wooden-framed windows and more countertop clutter, creating strong contrast between sunlight and the dim interior. Emphasize realistic documentary photography, richly textured wood, aged surfaces, retro packaging design, quiet everyday atmosphere, and a composition that leads the eye from the woman at center-left to the sewing machine and shelves at upper right.
